title: Agropyron pertenue
intro: Agropyron pertenue, also known as sweet grass, is a perennial grass native to North America, prized for its sweet, fragrant, and earthy scent. The plant has been traditionally used for its health benefits, including its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory properties, which have been shown to aid in digestion and promote wound healing. The bioactive constituents of sweet grass include sesquiterpenes, flavonoids, and phenolic acids, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Sweet grass is commonly used in herbal preparations such as teas, tinctures, and essential oils, which are often utilized for their calming and antiseptic effects.
taxonomy: Agropyron pertenue is a species of grass. It belongs to the family Poaceae, commonly known as the grass family. More specifically, Agropyron pertenue is classified within the subfamily Pooideae and tribe Triticeae. The genus Agropyron is a group of grasses that are native to the Northern Hemisphere. Within this genus, Agropyron pertenue is one of several species of grasses that are characterized by their slender stems and panicles of spikelets.
